name: Manchu

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Manchu (manc1252).

notes: Skeleton. Manchu is a Tungusic language of northeastern China, written in the vertical Manchu alphabet adapted from the Mongolian script. Although the script is alphabetic, letter shapes are heavily positional (distinct initial/medial/final allographs), vowel-harmony-conditioned, and disambiguated only by diacritic points, so a reliable letter-to-IPA reading depends on word-level segmentation and a lexicon/romanization stratum that the engine does not bundle. Grapheme mapping over raw Mongolian-script code points would misread the connected forms, so graphemes are left empty and coverage is effectively lexicon-dependent. The gold here is transcribed from the narrow Mollendorff-style tradition rather than derived from the script directly.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Manchu (manc1252)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
